qtype: Completing table of values, plotting cubic graph, solving equations graphically
tex:
The table shows some values for $y = x^{3} + 3x^{2} + 2$.
\newline

\begin{tabular}{c c c c c c}
$x$ & $-3.5$ & $-3$ & $-2.5$ & $-2$ & $-1.5$ \\
$y$ & $-4.1$ &  & $-2.1$ &  & $2.6$ \\
$x$ & $-1$ & $-0.5$ & $0$ & $0.5$ & $1$ \\
$y$ &  & $0.5$ &  & $1.5$ &  \\
$x$ & $1.5$ &  &  &  &  \\
$y$ & $12.1$ &  &  &  &  \\
\end{tabular}

(a) Complete the table.
\newline

(b) On the grid, draw the graph of $y = x^{3} + 3x^{2} + 2$ for $-3.5 \leq x \leq 1.5$.
\newline

(c) Use your graph to solve the equation $x^{3} + 3x^{2} + 2 = 0$ for $-3.5 \leq x \leq 1.5$.
\newline

(d) By drawing a suitable straight line, solve the equation $x^{3} + 3x^{2} + 2x + 2 = 0$ for $-3.5 \leq x \leq 1.5$.
\newline

(e) For $-3.5 \leq x \leq 1.5$, the equation $x^{3} + 3x^{2} + 2 = k$ has three solutions and $k$ is an integer.
\newline

Write down a possible value of $k$.
\newline

tex:
$\xi = \{students\ in\ a\ school\}$

$F = \{students\ who\ play\ football\}$

$B = \{students\ who\ play\ baseball\}$

There are 240 students in the school.
\newline

120 students play football.
\newline

40 students play baseball.
\newline

90 students play football but not baseball.
\newline

(a) Complete the Venn diagram to show this information.
\newline

(b) Find $n(F \cup B)$.
\newline

(c) A student in the school is chosen at random.
\newline

Find the probability that this student plays baseball but not football.
\newline

(d) Two students who play baseball are chosen at random.
\newline

Find the probability that they both also play football.
\newline

content.content:
Complete the Venn diagram to show this information.
\newline

Find $n(F \cup B)$.
\newline

A student in the school is chosen at random.
\newline

Find the probability that this student plays baseball but not football.
\newline

Two students who play baseball are chosen at random.
\newline

Find the probability that they both also play football.
\newline

$\xi = \{students\ in\ a\ school\}$

90 students play football but not baseball.

\begin{parts}

\relinput{Figures/Q06-Venn.tex}

\item Find $n(F \cup B)$.

\AnswerLine[]
\Marks{1}

\item A student in the school is chosen at random.

Find the probability that this student plays baseball but not football.

\AnswerLine[]
\Marks{1}

\item Two students who play baseball are chosen at random.

Find the probability that they both also play football.

\AnswerLine[]
\Marks{3}
\end{parts}

tex:
Brad travelled from his home in New York to Chamonix.
\newline

He left his home at 16 30 and travelled by taxi to the airport in New York.
\newline

This journey took 55 minutes and had an average speed of 18 km/h.
\newline

He then travelled by plane to Geneva, departing from New York at 22 15.
\newline

The flight path can be taken as an arc of a circle of radius 6400 km with a sector angle of 55.5°.
\newline

The local time in Geneva is 6 hours ahead of the local time in New York.
\newline

Brad arrived in Geneva at 11 25 the next day.
\newline

To complete his journey, Brad travelled by bus from Geneva to Chamonix.
\newline

This journey started at 13 00 and took 1 hour 36 minutes.
\newline

The average speed was 65 km/h.
\newline

The local time in Chamonix is the same as the local time in Geneva.
\newline

Find the overall average speed of Brad's journey from his home in New York to Chamonix.
\newline

Show all your working and give your answer in km/h.
\newline

 km/h

stem.content:
Brad travelled from his home in New York to Chamonix.
\newline

He left his home at 16 30 and travelled by taxi to the airport in New York.
\newline

This journey took 55 minutes and had an average speed of 18 km/h.
\newline

He then travelled by plane to Geneva, departing from New York at 22 15.
\newline

The flight path can be taken as an arc of a circle of radius 6400 km with a sector angle of 55.5°.
\newline

The local time in Geneva is 6 hours ahead of the local time in New York.
\newline

Brad arrived in Geneva at 11 25 the next day.
\newline

To complete his journey, Brad travelled by bus from Geneva to Chamonix.
\newline

This journey started at 13 00 and took 1 hour 36 minutes.
\newline

The average speed was 65 km/h.
\newline

The local time in Chamonix is the same as the local time in Geneva.
\newline

Find the overall average speed of Brad's journey from his home in New York to Chamonix.
\newline

Show all your working and give your answer in km/h.
\newline

_rawBody:
Brad travelled from his home in New York to Chamonix.

He left his home at 16 30 and travelled by taxi to the airport in New York.

This journey took 55 minutes and had an average speed of 18 km/h.

He then travelled by plane to Geneva, departing from New York at 22 15.

The flight path can be taken as an arc of a circle of radius 6400 km with a sector angle of 55.5°.

The local time in Geneva is 6 hours ahead of the local time in New York.

Brad arrived in Geneva at 11 25 the next day.

To complete his journey, Brad travelled by bus from Geneva to Chamonix.

This journey started at 13 00 and took 1 hour 36 minutes.

The average speed was 65 km/h.

The local time in Chamonix is the same as the local time in Geneva.

Find the overall average speed of Brad's journey from his home in New York to Chamonix.

Show all your working and give your answer in km/h.

\AnswerLine[] km/h
\Marks{11}
